headline: Option traders moderately bearish in Nike with shares down 1.92%
summary: Option traders moderately bearish in Nike (NKE), with shares down $1.07 near $54.63. Options volume roughly in line with average with 55k contracts traded and calls leading puts for a put/call ratio of 0.52, compared to a typical level near 0.71. Implied volatility (IV30) is higher by 0.8 points near 50.3, in the highest 10% of observations over the past year, suggesting an expected daily move of $1.73. Put-call skew steepened, indicating increased demand for downside protection, following an up

headline: U.S. companies should give Trump tariff refunds to workers as bonuses or raises: Greer
summary: The United States government could end up paying $165 billion of more in refunds for Trump's IEEPA tariffs that the Supreme Court ruled were illegal.

headline: Nike stock price gets reset by Barclays
summary: For months, years, investors have treated Nike (NKE) like a fading giant. A 62-year-old American athletic footwear and apparel corporation headquartered near Beaverton, Oregon. Its stock closed just under $55 on March 12, down nearly 25% in the last 12 months and down 14% year-to-date. But now one ...
